title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
hook: Imagine teaching an agent by showing it sequences of successes, not just rewards.
promise: You will understand how Decision Transformer reframes reinforcement learning as a sequence modeling problem using transformers.
prerequisite_note: It helps to know how agents learn from rewards and how transformers model sequences.

glossary.plain: Learning patterns in ordered data, like predicting the next word in a sentence.
A neural network architecture designed to handle sequences using attention mechanisms.
The sum of future rewards an agent aims to achieve from a given state.
Training agents using fixed datasets of past experiences instead of live interactions.
A sequence of states, actions, and rewards experienced by an agent.
A technique to ensure models only use past and present information, not the future.

cards.title: Teach RL like language
body: Discover how Decision Transformer treats learning to act as learning to predict sequences.

cards.title: What if RL was like autocomplete?
body: Imagine you want an agent to achieve a goal, but instead of telling it step by step, you show it examples of success.
What if training an agent was more like training a language model, learning from stories of past achievements rather than just chasing rewards?

cards.title: How sequence modeling changes RL
body: Traditionally, RL agents learn to maximize rewards by trial and error, updating their policies as they go.
Decision Transformer flips the script: it models the entire trajectory as a sequence, predicting actions that lead to desired outcomes.
cards.caption: The agent learns to act by predicting sequences that end in high rewards.

cards.title: Compare classic RL with Decision Transformer
body: How do agents learn differently when you swap value updates for sequence prediction?
cards.offNote: Agent updates its policy step by step, needs careful reward design, struggles with offline data.
cards.onNote: Agent learns from full trajectories, can specify target returns, leverages powerful sequence models.

cards.title: Recasting RL as sequence prediction
body: Instead of learning a value function or a policy directly, Decision Transformer predicts the next action given past states, actions, and desired returns.
It treats the RL problem like language modeling, where the sequence is a story of states, actions, and rewards, and the agent learns to write new successful stories.

cards.title: How much future do you target?
body: Adjust the importance of the target return to see how the agent's behavior changes.
cards.low: Agent aims for modest outcomes, often playing it safe.
cards.mid: Agent balances risk and reward, seeking reasonable success.
cards.high: Agent takes bold actions to maximize long-term rewards.

cards.title: What makes a Decision Transformer?
body: Select the building blocks that turn a transformer into a reinforcement learning agent.

choices.label: Trajectory dataset
choices.note: Provides the sequences of states, actions, and rewards.

choices.label: Transformer model
choices.note: Learns to model sequences and dependencies.

choices.label: Return-to-go input
choices.note: Specifies the future reward the agent should aim for.

choices.label: Causal masking
choices.note: Prevents the model from peeking at future actions or rewards.

choices.label: Offline RL setup
choices.note: Trains entirely from previously collected data.

choices.label: Sequence prediction objective
choices.note: Trains the model to predict the next action in the sequence.

cards.title: How well does it work?
body: Decision Transformer matches or outperforms traditional RL and imitation learning on several Atari and robotic control benchmarks.
It shows that sequence models can be powerful RL agents, especially when working with large offline datasets.

bars.label: Offline RL
bars.note: Ability to learn from static data

cards.caption: Decision Transformer shows strong results across classic RL and imitation learning settings.

cards.title: Why this paper is a landmark
body: Decision Transformer opened a new path by unifying reinforcement learning and sequence modeling, making RL more flexible and data-driven.
Its success inspired a wave of research using transformers for control and planning, making it a cornerstone in modern AI.

recap.label: Collect
recap.note: Use past trajectories of states, actions, and rewards.

recap.label: Sequence
recap.note: Treat RL as a sequence modeling problem.

recap.label: Predict
recap.note: Generate actions conditioned on target returns.

recap.label: Generalize
recap.note: Apply to new goals using the same model.

cards.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
body: Read the original work to dive deeper into the details and experiments.
